     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→
     5→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     7→
     8→## 2. Import Statement
     9→
    10→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
    11→- [x] No other imports are present.
    12→
    13→## 3. Module Docstring
    14→
    15→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
    16→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1002`.
    17→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1002`.
    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines $f(\alpha, n)$ and asks about the existence of an asymptotic distribution function).
    19→- [x] Optional: bibliography entry for Kesten [Ke60] included.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1002 ... end Erdos1002`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1002`).
    25→- [x] `open` statements (`open Finset Filter MeasureTheory`) appear **before** the namespace block (line 40).
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] Helper `def erdosF` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 44–45).
    30→- [x] Definition marked `noncomputable` (line 46).
    31→- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
    32→- [x] Function returning `ℝ` uses `lowerCamelCase` (`erdosF`).
    33→- N/A: No local notation used (none needed).
    34→- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).
    35→
    36→## 6. Theorem Statements
    37→
    38→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 60).
    39→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1002` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    40→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
    41→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11 28]` (line 59).
    42→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 49–58).
    43→- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[Er64b]`.
    44→
    45→### `answer()` usage
    46→
    47→The problem asks "Does $f(\alpha, n)$ have an asymptotic distribution function?" — a yes/no question (**Case 1**).
    48→
    49→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 60).
    50→- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as asked: existence of a monotone $g$ with the correct limit properties and convergence of the measure.
    51→- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ g`, `∀ c`) are on the RHS of `↔`, **after** `answer(sorry)` — not as theorem parameters.
    52→
    53→## 7. Category Attribute
    54→
    55→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute on the theorem.
    56→- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
    57→- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    58→
    59→## 8. AMS Attribute
    60→
    61→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute on the theorem.
    62→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: `11`, `28`).
    63→- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order** (11 < 28).
    64→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    65→- [x] Both `11` (Number theory) and `28` (Measure and integration) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    66→
    67→## 9. Proof Bodies
    68→
    69→- [x] Open conjecture uses `by sorry` (multi-line form `by\n    sorry` at lines 67–68, which is acceptable).
    70→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    71→- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
    72→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    73→
    74→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    75→
    76→- N/A: No variants present; single main theorem only.
    77→
    78→## 11. Code Quality
    79→
    80→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    81→- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`∃`, `∀`, `∈`, `≤`, `→`, `↔`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`).
    82→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    83→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    84→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    85→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    86→
    87→## 12. Build Verification
    88→
    89→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
    90→- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.
    91→
    92→## Summary
    93→
    94→**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)
    95→
    96→All stylistic, structural, and semantic checklist items pass. The formalization correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question (Case 1) and uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` accordingly. The definition `erdosF` cleanly captures the summatory function, and the theorem statement faithfully encodes the existence of an asymptotic distribution function via monotonicity, boundary limits, and measure-theoretic convergence. AMS codes 11 (Number theory) and 28 (Measure and integration) are appropriate for this problem.

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1002.lean`

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1002`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1002`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines $f(\alpha, n)$ and asks about the existence of an asymptotic distribution function).
- [x] Optional: bibliography entry for Kesten [Ke60] included.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1002 ... end Erdos1002`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1002`).
- [x] `open` statements (`open Finset Filter MeasureTheory`) appear **before** the namespace block (line 40).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] Helper `def erdosF` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 44–45).
- [x] Definition marked `noncomputable` (line 46).
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] Function returning `ℝ` uses `lowerCamelCase` (`erdosF`).
- N/A: No local notation used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 60).
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1002` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 11 28]` (line 59).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 49–58).
- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[Er64b]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks "Does $f(\alpha, n)$ have an asymptotic distribution function?" — a yes/no question (**Case 1**).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (line 60).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as asked: existence of a monotone $g$ with the correct limit properties and convergence of the measure.
- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ g`, `∀ c`) are on the RHS of `↔`, **after** `answer(sorry)` — not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute on the theorem.
- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute on the theorem.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: `11`, `28`).
- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order** (11 < 28).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both `11` (Number theory) and `28` (Measure and integration) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses `by sorry` (multi-line form `by\n    sorry` at lines 67–68, which is acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present; single main theorem only.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used (`∃`, `∀`, `∈`, `≤`, `→`, `↔`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

All stylistic, structural, and semantic checklist items pass. The formalization correctly identifies the problem as a yes/no question (Case 1) and uses `answer(sorry) ↔ P` accordingly. The definition `erdosF` cleanly captures the summatory function, and the theorem statement faithfully encodes the existence of an asymptotic distribution function via monotonicity, boundary limits, and measure-theoretic convergence. AMS codes 11 (Number theory) and 28 (Measure and integration) are appropriate for this problem.
